title: Why is episode 16 so awful looking
display_title: Why is episode 16 so awful looking
wikitext:
[]: Why is episode 16 so awful-looking? Post by Seen » Tue Dec 27, 2011 4:56 am I understand that the original masters were lost by the time they remastered the series, but THAT was the best they could do? I find that hard to believe. It looked like someone put the contrast and brightness settings on max or something. It really sucks, too, because that episode's animation was directed by Shinya Hasegawa, my favorite artist in Eva. If and when they release the show on Blu-Ray, will they work on it again? I'd assume the past 8 years has made technology advanced enough to make it look better.

[]: Post by Sachi » Tue Dec 27, 2011 5:27 am The animation in Evangelion is terribly inconsistent, and I don't put it past them to screw up even when remastering the series. Though, I'm not quite sure what you're talking about exactly. Can you provide any screenshots?

[]: Post by Tarnsman » Tue Dec 27, 2011 5:40 am It could be worse, it could be episode 11.

[]: Post by Reichu » Tue Dec 27, 2011 6:00 am Sachi wrote: The animation in Evangelion is terribly inconsistent, and I don't put it past them to screw up even when remastering the series. Though, I'm not quite sure what you're talking about exactly.

[]: Post by Xana » Tue Dec 27, 2011 6:08 am Seen wrote: when they release the show on Blu-Ray.

[]: Post by Seen » Tue Dec 27, 2011 7:09 am Xana wrote: NGE.... on Blu-Rei??... *bursts out laughing* Somehow I cannot see that happening

[]: Post by driftking18594 » Tue Dec 27, 2011 10:05 am Asuka still looks hot. It's a shame she was still only 13 then. :/

[]: Re: Why is episode 16 so awful-looking? Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Dec 27, 2011 11:29 am Seen wrote: Why is episode 16 so awful-looking?
